entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop used as the host in field experiments to evaluate the effects of nitrogen-fixing bacteria

entities.name: NITROGEN-FIXING BACTERIA
entities.description: Microorganisms used to inoculate pearl millet cultivars to potentially increase yields and nitrogen uptake

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: The research methodology employed at the ICRISAT Centre to observe interactions between host cultivars and bacterial strains

entities.name: HYDERABAD, INDIA
entities.description: The geographic location of the ICRISAT Centre where the research was conducted

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: An agricultural trait measuring the amount of grain produced, which increased in certain cultivars following inoculation

entities.name: DRY MATTER YIELD
entities.description: An agricultural trait measuring the total biomass produced, which increased in certain cultivars following inoculation

entities.name: NITROGEN UPTAKE
entities.description: A physiological trait measuring the amount of nitrogen absorbed by the plant, which increased up to 14.9 kg ha−1

entities.name: GRAIN NITROGEN CONTENT
entities.description: A quality trait measuring the nitrogen levels within the grain, which increased due to inoculation

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The method of introducing nitrogen-fixing bacteria into the pearl millet cultivars
